mechanism: Inhibits ABL and other kinases including FLT3/FGFR to block downstream survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved in CML/ALL; described as hitting FLT3 and downstream signaling nodes

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases, reducing pro-survival signaling in subsets of AML
additional_notes: Approved; text notes AML line sensitivity in subsets

mechanism: Blocks JAK/STAT pathway signaling involved in cytokine-supported AML growth
additional_notes: Approved in myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPN); combinations described as common

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to suppress MAPK pathway signaling, a survival/bypass route in AML
additional_notes: Approved; MAPK noted as key AML survival axis and bypass route from FLT3 inhibition

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce PI3K/AKT/mTOR-driven proliferation
additional_notes: Approved; rationale notes PI3K/AKT/mTOR frequently active in AML

mechanism: Allosteric mTORC1 inhibition to reduce growth and proliferation signaling
additional_notes: Also referred to as Rapamycin (alternative name given as 'Sirolimus / Rapamycin'); described as a common repurposing comparator

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to suppress cell growth/proliferation
additional_notes: Approved; noted to have AML preclinical literature

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic proteins BCL-2 and BCL-XL to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Described as clinical/approved in some regions; toxicity concerns clinically mentioned

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering chromatin/transcription and reducing AML cell growth
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; rationale emphasizes epigenetic dependency in AML

mechanism: Potent HDAC inhibition causing transcriptional changes and antiproliferative effects in hematologic malignancies
additional_notes: Approved; described as potent class member

mechanism: Purine nucleoside analog that disrupts DNA synthesis/repair to suppress proliferation
additional_notes: Approved; mentioned as used in AML regimens in some settings

mechanism: Impedes lysosomal acidification/autophagic flux, which can reduce survival/tolerance in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved; often explored in combination screens; rationale notes autophagy supports AML survival and drug tolerance
